activation_policy: activate when figure/caption/formal artifacts are touched
completion_gate: S11FigureCaptionArtifactBundle validates S08 contract compliance, source-data trace, panel/caption claim traces, image integrity, visual polish policy/report, accessibility, export manifest, CandidateArtifactReturn, and authority boundary without final export or completion claims
purpose: Produce contract-bound candidate figures, captions, tables, algorithms, formulas, render plans, and export bundles without changing proof role, evidence meaning, or claim boundaries.

subagent_lane_policy.rationale: Artifact production is often deterministic but paper-facing; independent QA is required when it becomes final/export-facing.

capability_adapters.authority_boundary: S11 remains controller-owned; adapter is helper only

stage_quality_contract.active_profile_source: S08 visual/formal contract, S09B artifact packet, S10 text callout context, current LaTeX slots, and active paper profile; no global venue/profile default
producer_return_obligations: artifact/caption candidate bundle bound to S08 proof role and S09B packet
source_data_trace and panel_claim_trace for every evidential panel
caption_claim_trace tied to S04 claims and S10 text callout context
current_latex_slots mapping for figure environment, label, caption, placement, and callout references
editable_source_bundle plus rendered artifact or render_plan_if_not_rendered
CandidateArtifactReturn and verifier_evidence without final export or completion claim

checks: S08 proof role and caption boundary are preserved
S10 text callout context and current_latex_slots are consumed rather than guessed
source data, panel claims, and caption claims are traceable and do not create unsupported claims
visual polish does not alter evidence meaning or proof role
nature-figure direct-call output remains an S11 helper artifact, not completion authority
final export/submission claims remain outside S11

downstream_design_force: S12 receives artifact/caption bundle with source-data, panel-claim, caption-claim, and LaTeX-slot traces
S13 can review figure/caption risks against S08, S10 callouts, and current LaTeX placement
S16 and RenderedManuscriptAuditGate can verify rendered figure/caption presence, references, and no template-only visual handoff

failure_severity_policy.BLOCKING: source data, proof role, caption boundary, S10 callout context, or current_latex_slots are missing for a paper-facing artifact
failure_severity_policy.MAJOR: artifact/caption changes claim meaning, proof role, or evidence interpretation, or lacks verifier evidence
failure_severity_policy.MINOR: local accessibility, label, manifest, or polish-report defect without meaning drift
failure_severity_policy.WATCH: non-blocking readability or reviewer-risk concern tracked for S13/S16 attention

stage_quality_contract.nearest_responsible_stage_policy: S11 owns artifact/caption production defects; missing visual contract routes to S08, source-data gaps to S01/S08, text-callout gaps to S10/S09B.
stage_quality_contract.anti_over_strictness_boundary: MINOR/WATCH accessibility or polish findings may be repaired locally; do not rerun S04-S10 unless evidence meaning, proof role, or callout binding is wrong.
